hypothesis: If large language models (particularly reasoning-optimized models like o4-mini) are prompted with one-shot in-context examples of increasing complexity, then they will achieve higher precision, recall, and F1-scores in extracting PROV-DM-compliant provenance graphs from materials synthesis literature compared to zero-shot prompting and less capable models, because complex examples better constrain the structured output format and domain-specific terminology required for accurate graph construction.
